attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Pediculosis due to Pediculus humanus capitis

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~6 years from reported body weight ~22 kg and 'young child' framing — no explicit age in the source note; confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred female from consistent she/her pronouns throughout the source note.
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00035 — SEQ derived from source CIA-035; ingest --reseq / maintainer to confirm or reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.
Primary specialty coded DERM; could reasonably be tagged ID (ectoparasitic infestation) or PAEDS — flagged for reviewer preference.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person urgent home visit with direct scalp examination, palpation of cervical lymph nodes, clinician-visualised live lice/nits, and measured vitals. Caregiver-observable findings (adherent specks, movement, excoriations, weight, 'not feverish') moved to 01.objective_data_offered; clinician-only findings (definitive scalp/dermoscopy confirmation, tender posterior cervical lymphadenopathy, measured vitals) sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits.

Over-triage de-anchoring: the source escalated to an urgent after-hours home visit citing MBS urgent item numbers 585-600. Baseline tier set to T1 on true clinical acuity (benign, telehealth/pharmacist-manageable), not the source's distress/billing-driven urgency framing.

Paediatric sedating antihistamine (promethazine, named in the source for 2-3 nights) recorded WITHOUT a dose: no paediatric dose in the source and promethazine carries paediatric respiratory-depression cautions — flagged for in-person/pharmacist review rather than dosed (no-paediatric-dose rule, protocol §8).
AMT/PBS medicine codes for permethrin, malathion, dimeticone and promethazine were NOT assigned — omitted rather than fabricated; pending terminology receipt.
All SNOMED CT / ICD-10-AM codes are candidate values, unverified pending terminology receipt; SNOMED codes for differential diagnoses are best-effort candidates.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
